" --append-system-prompt "You are an AI issue solver. You prefer to find the root cause of each and every issue. When you talk, you prefer to speak with facts which you have double-checked yourself or cite sources that provide evidence, like quote actual code or give references to documents or pages found on the internet. You are polite and patient, and prefer to assume good intent, trying your best to be helpful. If you are unsure or have assumptions, you prefer to test them yourself or ask questions to clarify requirements.
General guidelines.
   - When you execute commands, always save their logs to files for easier reading if the output becomes large.
   - When running commands, do not set a timeout yourself — let them run as long as needed (default timeout - 2 minutes is more than enough), and once they finish, review the logs in the file.
   - When running sudo commands (especially package installations like apt-get, yum, npm install, etc.), always run them in the background to avoid timeout issues and permission errors when the process needs to be killed. Use the run_in_background parameter or append & to the command.
   - When CI is failing or user reports failures, consider adding a detailed investigation protocol to your todo list with these steps:
      Step 1: List recent runs with timestamps using: gh run list --repo linksplatform/trees-rs --branch issue-20-f92f7ec937ab --limit 5 --json databaseId,conclusion,createdAt,headSha
      Step 2: Verify runs are after the latest commit by checking timestamps and SHA
      Step 3: For each non-passing run, download logs to preserve them: gh run view {run-id} --repo linksplatform/trees-rs --log > ci-logs/{workflow}-{run-id}.log
      Step 4: Read each downloaded log file using Read tool to understand the actual failures
      Step 5: Report findings with specific errors and line numbers from logs
      This detailed investigation is especially helpful when user mentions CI failures, asks to investigate logs, you see non-passing status, or when finalizing a PR.
      Note: If user says \"failing\" but tools show \"passing\", this might indicate stale data - consider downloading fresh logs and checking timestamps to resolve the discrepancy.
   - When a code or log file has more than 1500 lines, read it in chunks of 1500 lines.
   - When facing a complex problem, do as much tracing as possible and turn on all verbose modes.
   - When you create debug, test, or example/experiment scripts for fixing, always keep them in an ./examples and/or ./experiments folders so you can reuse them later.
   - When testing your assumptions, use the experiment scripts, and add it to ./experiments folder.
   - When your experiments can show real world use case of the software, add it to ./examples folder.
   - When you face something extremely hard, use divide and conquer — it always helps.

Initial research.
   - When you start, make sure you create detailed plan for yourself and follow your todo list step by step, make sure that as many points from these guidelines are added to your todo list to keep track of everything that can help you solve the issue with highest possible quality.
   - When user mentions CI failures or asks to investigate logs, consider adding these todos to track the investigation: (1) List recent CI runs with timestamps, (2) Download logs from failed runs to ci-logs/ directory, (3) Analyze error messages and identify root cause, (4) Implement fix, (5) Verify fix resolves the specific errors found in logs.
   - When you read issue, read all details and comments thoroughly.
   - When you see screenshots or images in issue descriptions, pull request descriptions, comments, or discussions, download the image to a local file first, then use Read tool to view and analyze it. IMPORTANT: Before reading downloaded images with the Read tool, verify the file is a valid image (not HTML). Use a CLI tool like 'file' command to check the actual file format. Reading corrupted or non-image files (like GitHub's \"Not Found\" pages saved as .png) can cause \"Could not process image\" errors and will crash the AI solver process. If the file command shows \"HTML\", \"text\", or \"ASCII text\", the download FAILED — do NOT call Read on this file. Instead: (1) For images from GitHub issues/PRs (URLs containing \"github.com/user-attachments\"), these require authentication — retry with: curl -L -H \"Authorization: token \$(gh auth token)\" -o <filename> \"<url>\" (2) If retry still fails, skip the image and note it was unavailable.
   - When you need issue details, use gh issue view https://github.com/linksplatform/trees-rs/issues/20.
   - When you need related code, use gh search code --owner linksplatform [keywords].
   - When you need repo context, read files in your working directory.
   - When you study related work, study the most recent related pull requests.
   - When issue is not defined enough, write a comment to ask clarifying questions.
   - When accessing GitHub Gists (especially private ones), use gh gist view command instead of direct URL fetching to ensure proper authentication.
   - When you are fixing a bug, please make sure you first find the actual root cause, do as many experiments as needed.
   - When you are fixing a bug and code does not have enough tracing/logs, add them and make sure they stay in the code, but are switched off by default.
   - When you need comments on a pull request, note that GitHub has THREE different comment types with different API endpoints:
      1. PR review comments (inline code comments): gh api repos/linksplatform/trees-rs/pulls/21/comments --paginate
      2. PR conversation comments (general discussion): gh api repos/linksplatform/trees-rs/issues/21/comments --paginate
      3. PR reviews (approve/request changes): gh api repos/linksplatform/trees-rs/pulls/21/reviews --paginate
      IMPORTANT: The command \"gh pr view --json comments\" ONLY returns conversation comments and misses review comments!
   - When you need latest comments on issue, use gh api repos/linksplatform/trees-rs/issues/20/comments --paginate.

Solution development and testing.
   - When issue is solvable, implement code with tests.
   - When implementing features, search for similar existing implementations in the codebase and use them as examples instead of implementing everything from scratch.

        "content": "[![Unlicense](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-Unlicense-blue.svg)](http://unlicense.org/)\n[![Crates.io](https://img.shields.io/crates/v/platform-trees?label=crates.io&style=flat)](https://crates.io/crates/platform-trees)\n[![CI/CD Pipeline](https://github.com/linksplatform/trees-rs/workflows/CI%2FCD%20Pipeline/badge.svg)](https://github.com/linksplatform/trees-rs/actions?workflow=CI%2FCD+Pipeline)\n[![Docs.rs](https://docs.rs/platform-trees/badge.svg)](https://docs.rs/platform-trees)\n\n# [Trees](https://github.com/linksplatform/trees-rs) for Rust\n\nLinksPlatform's `platform-trees` crate — tree and linked list data\nstructure traits for the Links platform.\n\nCrates.io package: [platform-trees](https://crates.io/crates/platform-trees)\n\n## Overview\n\nThis crate provides generic traits for size-balanced binary trees and\ncircular linked lists used throughout the LinksPlatform ecosystem:\n\n- **`RecursiveSizeBalancedTree<T>`** — Base size-balanced binary tree\n  trait with node navigation, tree rotations, size management, and\n  tree queries (`contains`, `get_leftest`, `get_rightest`).\n- **`IterativeSizeBalancedTree<T>`** — Extends\n  `RecursiveSizeBalancedTree` with iterative `attach` and `detach`\n  operations that avoid stack overflow on deep trees.\n- **`LinkedList<T>`** — Base doubly-linked list trait with\n  `get_previous`, `get_next`, `set_previous`, `set_next`.\n- **`AbsoluteLinkedList<T>`** — Linked list with direct access to\n  `first` and `last` elements and size tracking.\n- **`RelativeLinkedList<T>`** — Linked list with head-relative\n  positioning, supporting multiple independent lists sharing storage.\n- **`AbsoluteCircularLinkedList<T>`** — Circular doubly-linked list\n  with absolute positioning: `attach_before`, `attach_after`,\n  `attach_as_first`, `attach_as_last`, `detach`.\n- **`RelativeCircularLinkedList<T>`** — Circular doubly-linked list\n  with head-relative positioning, supporting multiple circular lists\n  in shared storage.\n\n## Installation\n\nAdd to your `Cargo.toml`:\n\n```toml\n[dependencies]\nplatform-trees = \"0.1.0-beta.1\"\n```\n\n## Usage\n\n### Implementing `RecursiveSizeBalancedTree`\n\n```rust\nuse platform_trees::{IterativeSizeBalancedTree, RecursiveSizeBalancedTree};\n\nstruct MyTreeStorage {\n    nodes: Vec<Node>,\n}\n\nstruct Node {\n    left: usize,\n    right: usize,\n    size: usize,\n}\n\nimpl RecursiveSizeBalancedTree<usize> for MyTreeStorage {\n    unsafe fn get_mut_left_reference(&mut self, node: usize) -> *mut usize {\n        &mut self.nodes[node].left\n    }\n\n    unsafe fn get_mut_right_reference(&mut self, node: usize) -> *mut usize {\n        &mut self.nodes[node].right\n    }\n\n    unsafe fn get_left_reference(&self, node: usize) -> *const usize {\n        &self.nodes[node].left\n    }\n\n    unsafe fn get_right_reference(&self, node: usize) -> *const usize {\n        &self.nodes[node].right\n    }\n\n    unsafe fn get_left(&self, node: usize) -> usize {\n        self.nodes[node].left\n    }\n\n    unsafe fn get_right(&self, node: usize) -> usize {\n        self.nodes[node].right\n    }\n\n    unsafe fn get_size(&self, node: usize) -> usize {\n        self.nodes[node].size\n    }\n\n    unsafe fn set_left(&mut self, node: usize, left: usize) {\n        self.nodes[node].left = left;\n    }\n\n    unsafe fn set_right(&mut self, node: usize, right: usize) {\n        self.nodes[node].right = right;\n    }\n\n    unsafe fn set_size(&mut self, node: usize, size: usize) {\n        self.nodes[node].size = size;\n    }\n\n    unsafe fn first_is_to_the_left_of_second(&self, first: usize, second: usize) -> bool {\n        first < second\n    }\n\n    unsafe fn first_is_to_the_right_of_second(&self, first: usize, second: usize) -> bool {\n        first > second\n    }\n}\n\nimpl IterativeSizeBalancedTree<usize> for MyTreeStorage {}\n```\n\n### Implementing `LinkedList`\n\n```rust\nuse platform_trees::{LinkedList, AbsoluteLinkedList, AbsoluteCircularLinkedList};\n\nstruct MyListStorage {\n    elements: Vec<ListElement>,\n    first: usize,\n    last: usize,\n    size: usize,\n}\n\nstruct ListElement {\n    prev: usize,\n    next: usize,\n}\n\nimpl LinkedList<usize> for MyListStorage {\n    fn get_previous(&self, element: usize) -> usize {\n        self.elements[element].prev\n    }\n\n    fn get_next(&self, element: usize) -> usize {\n        self.elements[element].next\n    }\n\n    fn set_previous(&mut self, element: usize, previous: usize) {\n        self.elements[element].prev = previous;\n    }\n\n    fn set_next(&mut self, element: usize, next: usize) {\n        self.elements[element].next = next;\n    }\n}\n\nimpl AbsoluteLinkedList<usize> for MyListStorage {\n    fn get_first(&self) -> usize { self.first }\n    fn get_last(&self) -> usize { self.last }\n    fn get_size(&self) -> usize { self.size }\n\n    fn set_first(&mut self, element: usize) { self.first = element; }\n    fn set_last(&mut self, element: usize) { self.last = element; }\n    fn set_size(&mut self, size: usize) { self.size = size; }\n}\n\nimpl AbsoluteCircularLinkedList<usize> for MyListStorage {}\n```\n\n## Depend on\n\n- [num-traits](https://crates.io/crates/num-traits)\n- [platform-num](https://crates.io/crates/platform-num)\n  ([Numbers](https://github.com/linksplatform/Numbers))\n\n## Dependent libraries\n\n- [doublets](https://crates.io/crates/doublets)\n  ([doublets-rs](https://github.com/linksplatform/doublets-rs))\n\n## License\n\nThis crate is released to the **public domain** under the It is truly free as in freedom.",
        "is_error": false

name: Build release\n229\t        run: cargo build --release --verbose\n230\t\n231\t      - name: Check package\n232\t        run: cargo package --list\n233\t\n234\t  # === AUTO RELEASE ===\n235\t  # Automatic release on push to main using changelog fragments\n236\t  # This job automatically bumps version based on fragments in changelog.d/\n237\t  auto-release:\n238\t    name: Auto Release\n239\t    needs: [lint, test, build]\n240\t    # Note: always() ensures consistent behavior with other jobs that depend on jobs using always().\n241\t    if: |\n242\t      always() && !cancelled() &&\n243\t      github.event_name == 'push' &&\n244\t      github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' &&\n245\t      needs.build.result == 'success'\n246\t    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n247\t    permissions:\n248\t      contents: write\n249\t    steps:\n250\t      - name: Install rust-script\n259\t        run: cargo install rust-script\n260\t\n261\t      - name: Configure git\n262\t        run: rust-script scripts/git-config.rs\n263\t\n264\t      - name: Determine bump type from changelog fragments\n265\t        id: bump_type\n266\t        run: rust-script scripts/get-bump-type.rs\n267\t\n268\t      - name: Check if version already released or no fragments\n269\t        id: check\n270\t        env:\n271\t          HAS_FRAGMENTS: ${{ steps.bump_type.outputs.has_fragments }}\n272\t        run: rust-script scripts/check-release-needed.rs\n273\t\n274\t      - name: Collect changelog and bump version\n275\t        id: version\n276\t        if: steps.check.outputs.should_release == 'true' && steps.check.outputs.skip_bump != 'true'\n277\t        run: |\n278\t          rust-script scripts/version-and-commit.rs \\\n279\t            --bump-type \"${{ steps.bump_type.outputs.bump_type }}\"\n280\t\n281\t      - name: Get current version\n282\t        id: current_version\n283\t        if: steps.check.outputs.should_release == 'true'\n284\t        run: rust-script scripts/get-version.rs\n285\t\n286\t      - name: Build release\n287\t        if: steps.check.outputs.should_release == 'true'\n288\t        run: cargo build --release\n289\t\n290\t      - name: Publish to Crates.io\n291\t        if: steps.check.outputs.should_release == 'true'\n292\t        id: publish-crate\n293\t        env:\n294\t          CARGO_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CARGO_TOKEN }}\n295\t        run: rust-script scripts/publish-crate.rs\n296\t\n297\t      - name: Create GitHub Release\n298\t        if: steps.check.outputs.should_release == 'true'\n299\t        env:\n300\t          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n301\t        run: rust-script scripts/create-github-release.rs --release-version \"${{ steps.current_version.outputs.version }}\" --repository \"${{ github.repository }}\"\n302\t\n303\t  # === MANUAL INSTANT RELEASE ===\n304\t  # Manual release via workflow_dispatch - only after CI passes\n305\t  manual-release:\n306\t    name: Instant Release\n307\t    needs: [lint, test, build]\n308\t    # Note: always() is required to evaluate the condition when dependencies use always().\n309\t    # The build job ensures lint and test passed before this job runs.\n310\t    if: |\n311\t      always() && !cancelled() &&\n312\t      github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' &&\n313\t      github.event.inputs.release_mode == 'instant' &&\n314\t      needs.build.result == 'success'\n315\t    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n316\t    permissions:\n317\t      contents: write\n318\t    steps:\n319\t      - uses: actions/checkout@v6\n320\t        with:\n321\t          fetch-depth: 0\n322\t          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n323\t\n324\t      - name: Setup Rust\n325\t        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable\n326\t\n327\t      - name: Version and commit\n337\t        id: version\n338\t        env:\n339\t          BUMP_TYPE: ${{ github.event.inputs.bump_type }}\n340\t          DESCRIPTION: ${{ github.event.inputs.description }}\n341\t        run: rust-script scripts/version-and-commit.rs --bump-type \"${{ github.event.inputs.bump_type }}\" --description \"${{ github.event.inputs.description }}\"\n342\t\n343\t      - name: Build release\n344\t        if: steps.version.outputs.version_committed == 'true' || steps.version.outputs.already_released == 'true'\n345\t        run: cargo build --release\n346\t\n347\t      - support)\n- Upgrade `actions/cache` from v4 to v5 (Node.js 24 support)\n- Upgrade `peter-evans/create-pull-request` from v7 to v8 (Node.js 24 support)\n---\nbump: patch\n---\n\n### Fixed\n- Fix inconsistent license: changed `license = \"LGPL-3.0\"` to `license = \"Unlicense\"` in `Cargo.toml` to match the `LICENSE` file and `README.md`\n---\nbump: patch\n---\n\n### Fixed\n- Fix CI/CD auto-release pipeline failing to parse pre-release semver versions (e.g., `0.1.0-beta.1`) in `Cargo.toml`\n- Updated version parsing regex in `version-and-commit.rs` and `bump-version.rs` to support optional pre-release suffixes\n---\nbump: patch\n---\n\n### Added\n- Integration tests verifying `funty()` method compatibility with downstream crates (doublets-rs, data-rs)\n- Compatibility analysis confirming safe removal of `funty` crate dependency (#15)\n# Changelog Fragments\n\nThis directory contains changelog fragments that will be collected into `CHANGELOG.md` during releases.\n\n## How to Add a Changelog Fragment\n\nWhen making changes that should be documented in the changelog, create a fragment file:\n\n```bash\n# Create a new fragment with timestamp\ntouch changelog.d/$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)_description.md\n\n# Or manually create a file matching the pattern: YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS_description.md\n```\n\n## Fragment Format\n\nEach fragment should include a **frontmatter section** specifying the version bump type:\n\n```markdown\n---\nbump: patch\n---\n\n### Fixed\n- Description of bug fix\n```\n\n### Bump Types\n\nUse semantic versioning bump types in the frontmatter:\n\n- **`major`**: Breaking changes (incompatible API changes)\n- **`minor`**: New features (backward compatible)\n- **`patch`**: Bug fixes (backward compatible)\n\n### Content Categories\n\nUse these categories in your fragment content:\n\n```markdown\n---\nbump: minor\n---\n\n### Added\n- Description of new feature\n\n### Changed\n- Description of change to existing functionality\n\n### Fixed\n- Description of bug fix\n\n### Removed\n- Description of removed feature\n\n### Deprecated\n- Description of deprecated feature\n\n### Security\n- Description of security fix\n```\n\n## Examples\n\n### Adding a new feature (minor bump)\n\n```markdown\n---\nbump: minor\n---\n\n### Added\n- New async processing mode for batch operations\n```\n\n### Fixing a bug (patch bump)\n\n```markdown\n---\nbump: patch\n---\n\n### Fixed\n- Fixed memory leak in connection pool handling\n```\n\n### Breaking change (major bump)\n\n```markdown\n---\nbump: major\n---\n\n### Changed\n- Renamed `process()` to `process_async()` - this is a breaking change\n\n### Removed\n- Removed deprecated `legacy_mode` option\n```\n\n## Why Fragments?\n\nUsing changelog fragments (similar to [Changesets](https://github.com/changesets/changesets) in JavaScript and [Scriv](https://scriv.readthedocs.io/) in Python):\n\n1. **No merge conflicts**: Multiple PRs can add fragments without conflicts\n2. **Per-PR documentation**: Each PR documents its own changes\n3. **Automated version bumping**: Version bump type is specified per-change\n4. **Automated collection**: Fragments are automatically collected during release\n5. **Consistent format**: Template ensures consistent changelog entries\n\n## How It Works\n\n1. **During PR**: Add a fragment file with your changes and bump type\n2. **On merge to main**: The release workflow automatically:\n   - Reads all fragment files and determines the highest bump type\n   - Bumps the version in `Cargo.toml` accordingly\n   - Collects fragments into `CHANGELOG.md`\n   - Creates a git tag and GitHub release\n   - Removes processed fragment files\n\n## Multiple PRs and Bump Priority\n\nWhen multiple PRs are merged before a release, all pending fragments are processed together. The **highest** bump type wins:\n\n- If any fragment specifies `major`, the release is a major version bump\n- Otherwise, if any specifies `minor`, the release is a minor version bump\n- Otherwise, the release is a patch version bump\n\nThis ensures that breaking changes are never missed, even when combined with smaller changes.\n\n## Default Behavior\n\nIf a fragment doesn't include a bump type in the frontmatter, it defaults to `patch`.",

args.iter().position(|a| a == &flag) {\n        return args.get(idx + 1).cloned();\n    }\n\n    let env_name = name.to_uppercase().replace('-', \"_\");\n    env::var(&env_name).ok().filter(|s|  }\n\n    if Path::new(\"./Cargo.toml\").exists() {\n        eprintln!(\"Detected single-language repository (Cargo.toml in root)\");\n        return \".\".to_string();\n    }\n\n    if {\n                Ok(String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).trim().to_string())\n            } else {\n                let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr);\n {\n    Command::new(command)\n        .args(args)\n        .output()\n        .map(|o| o.status.success())\n        .unwrap_or(false)\n}\n\nstruct Version {\n    major: u32,\n    minor: u32,\n    patch: u32,\n    #[allow(dead_code)]\n    pre_release: Option<String>,\n}\n\nimpl Version {\n    fn parse(content: &str) -> Option<Version> {\n        // Support semver pre-release versions like \"0.1.0-beta.1\"\n        let re = Regex::new(r#\"(?m)^version\\s*=\\s*\"(\\d+)\\.(\\d+)\\.(\\d+)(?:-([^\"]+))?\"\"#).ok()?;\n        let caps = re.captures(content)?;\n        Some(Version {\n            major: caps.get(1)?.as_str().parse().ok()?,\n            minor: caps.get(2)?.as_str().parse().ok()?,\n            patch: caps.get(3)?.as_str().parse().ok()?,\n            pre_release: caps.get(4).map(|m| m.as_str().to_string()),\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn bump(&self, bump_type: &str) -> String {\n        // If version has a pre-release suffix (e.g. 0.1.0-beta.1),\n        // a bump removes the suffix and bumps the core version.\n        // This follows semver convention: bumping a pre-release produces the next release.\n        match bump_type {\n            \"major\" => format!(\"{}.0.0\", self.major + 1),\n            \"minor\" => format!(\"{}.{}.0\", self.major, self.minor + 1),\n            _ => format!(\"{}.{}.{}\", self.major, self.minor, self.patch + 1),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nfn update_cargo_toml(cargo_toml_path: &str, new_version: &str) -> Result<(), String> {\n    let content = fs::read_to_string(cargo_toml_path)\n        .map_err(|e| format!(\"Failed to read {}: {}\", cargo_toml_path, e))?;\n\n    let re = Regex::new(r#\"(?m)^(version\\s*=\\s*\")[^\"]+(\")\"#).unwrap();\n    let new_content = re.replace(&content, format!(\"${{1}}{}${{2}}\", new_version).as_str());\n\n    fs::write(cargo_toml_path, new_content.as_ref())\n        .map_err(|e| format!(\"Failed to write {}: {}\", cargo_toml_path, e))?;\n\n    println!(\"Updated {} to version {}\", cargo_toml_path, new_version);\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn check_tag_exists(version: &str) -> bool {\n    exec_check(\"git\", &[\"rev-parse\", &format!(\"v{}\", version)])\n}\n\nfn caps.get(1).unwrap().as_str().trim().to_string()\n    } else {\n        content.trim().to_string()\n    }\n}\n\nfn collect_changelog(changelog_dir: &str, changelog_file: &str, version: &str) {\n    let dir_path = Path::new(changelog_dir);\n    if !dir_path.exists() {\n        return;\n    }\n\n    let mut files: Vec<_> = match fs::read_dir(dir_path) {\n        Ok(entries) => entries\n            .filter_map(|e| e.ok())\n            .map(|e| e.path())\n            .filter(|p| {\n                p.extension().map_or(false, |ext| ext == \"md\")\n                    && p.file_name().map_or(false, |name| name != \"README.md\")\n            })\n            .collect(),\n        Err(_) => return,\n    };\n\n    if files.is_empty() {\n        return;\n    }\n\n    files.sort();\n\n    let fragments: Vec<String> = files\n        .iter()\n        .filter_map(|f| fs::read_to_string(f).ok())\n        .map(|c| strip_frontmatter(&c))\n        .filter(|c| !c.is_empty())\n        .collect();\n\n    if fragments.is_empty() {\n        return;\n    }\n\n    let date_str = Utc::now().format(\"%Y-%m-%d\").to_string();\n    let new_entry = format!(\"\\n## [{}] - {}\\n\\n{}\\n\", version, date_str, fragments.join(\"\\n\\n\"));\n\n    if Path::new(changelog_file).exists() {\n        let mut content = fs::read_to_string(changelog_file).unwrap_or_default();\n        let lines: Vec<&str> = content.lines().collect();\n        let mut insert_index = None;\n\n        for (i, line) in lines.iter().enumerate() {\n            if line.starts_with(\"## [\") {\n                insert_index = Some(i);\n                break;\n            }\n        }\n\n        if let Some(idx) = insert_index {\n            let mut new_lines: Vec<String> = lines[..idx].iter().map(|s| s.to_string()).collect();\n            new_lines.push(new_entry.clone());\n            new_lines.extend(lines[idx..].iter().map(|s| s.to_string()));\n            content = new_lines.join(\"\\n\");\n        } else {\n            content.push_str(&new_entry);\n        }\n\n        fs::write(changelog_file, content).expect(\"Failed to write changelog\");\n    }\n\n    println!(\"Collected {} changelog fragment(s)\", files.len());\n}\n\nfn main() {\n    let bump_type = match get_arg(\"bump-type\") {\n        Some(bt) => bt,\n        None => {\n            eprintln!(\"Usage: rust-script scripts/version-and-commit.rs --bump-type <major|minor|patch> [--description <desc>] [--rust-root <path>]\");\n            exit(1);\n        }\n    };\n\n    if ![\"major\", \"minor\", \"patch\"].contains(&bump_type.as_str()) {\n        eprintln!(\"Invalid bump type: {}. Must be major, minor, or patch.\", bump_type);\n        exit(1);\n    }\n\n    let description = get_arg(\"description\");\n    let rust_root = get_rust_root();\n    let cargo_toml = get_cargo_toml_path(&rust_root);\n    let changelog_dir = get_changelog_dir(&rust_root);\n    let changelog_file = get_changelog_path(&rust_root);\n\n    // Configure git\n    let _ = exec(\"git\", &[\"config\", \"user.name\", \"github-actions[bot]\"]);\n    let _ = exec(\"git\", &[\"config\", \"user.email\", \"github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com\"]);\n\n    // Get current version\n    let content = match fs::read_to_string(&cargo_toml) {\n        Ok(c) => c,\n        Err(e) => {\n            eprintln!(\"Error reading {}: {}\", cargo_toml, e);\n            exit(1);\n        }\n    };\n\n    let current = match Version::parse(&content) {\n        Some(v) => v,\n        None => {\n            eprintln!(\"Error: Could not parse version from {}\", cargo_toml);\n            exit(1);\n        }\n    };\n\n    let new_version = current.bump(&bump_type);\n\n    // Check if this version was already released\n    if check_tag_exists(&new_version) {\n        println!(\"Tag v{} already exists\", new_version);\n        set_output(\"already_released\", \"true\");\n        set_output(\"new_version\", &new_version);\n        return;\n    }\n\n    // Update version in Cargo.toml\n    if let Err(e) = update_cargo_toml(&cargo_toml, &new_version) {\n        eprintln!(\"Error: {}\", e);\n        exit(1);\n    }\n\n    // Collect changelog fragments\n    collect_changelog(&changelog_dir, &changelog_file, &new_version);\n\n    // Stage Cargo.toml and CHANGELOG.md\n    let _ = exec(\"git\", &[\"add\", &cargo_toml, &changelog_file]);\n\n    // Check if there are changes to commit\n    if exec_check(\"git\", &[\"diff\", \"--cached\", \"--quiet\"]) {\n        // No changes to commit\n        println!(\"No changes to commit\");\n        set_output(\"version_committed\", \"false\");\n        set_output(\"new_version\", &new_version);\n        return;\n    }\n\n    // Commit changes\n    let commit_msg = match &description {\n        Some(desc) => format!(\"chore: release v{}\\n\\n{}\", new_version, desc),\n        None => format!(\"chore: release v{}\", new_version),\n    };\n\n    if let Err(e) = exec(\"git\", &[\"commit\", \"-m\", &commit_msg]) {\n        eprintln!(\"Error committing: {}\", e);\n        exit(1);\n    }\n    println!(\"Committed version {}\", new_version);\n\n    // Create tag\n    let tag_msg = match &description {\n        Some(desc) => format!(\"Release v{}\\n\\n{}\", new_version, desc),\n        None => format!(\"Release v{}\", new_version),\n    };\n\n    if let Err(e) = exec(\"git\", &[\"tag\", \"-a\", &format!(\"v{}\", new_version), \"-m\", &tag_msg]) {\n        eprintln!(\"Error creating tag: {}\", e);\n        exit(1);\n    }\n    println!(\"Created tag v{}\", new_version);\n\n    // Push changes and tag\n    if let Err(e) = exec(\"git\", &[\"push\"]) {\n        eprintln!(\"Error pushing: {}\", e);\n        exit(1);\n    }\n\n    if let Err(e) = exec(\"git\", &[\"push\", \"--tags\"]) {\n        eprintln!(\"Error pushing tags: {}\", e);\n        exit(1);\n    }\n    println!(\"Pushed changes and tags\");\n\n    set_output(\"version_committed\", \"true\");\n    set_output(\"new_version\", {\n                Ok(String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).trim().to_string())\n            } else {\n                let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr);\n   }\n    }\n}\n\nfn update_cargo_toml(cargo_toml_path: &str, new_version: &str) -> Result<(), String> {\n    let content = fs::read_to_string(cargo_toml_path)\n        .map_err(|e| format!(\"Failed to name: Check for changelog fragments\n        env:\n          GITHUB_BASE_REF: ${{ github.base_ref }}\n        run: rust-script scripts/check-changelog-fragment.rs\n\n  # === VERSION CHECK - prevents manual version modification in PRs ===\n  # This ensures versions are only modified by the automated release pipeline\n  version-check:\n    name: Version Modification Check\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v6\n        with:\n          fetch-depth: 0\n\n      - name: Setup Rust\n        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable\n\n      - name: Install rust-script\n        run: cargo install rust-script\n\n      - name: Check for manual version changes\n        env:\n          GITHUB_EVENT_NAME: ${{ github.event_name }}\n          GITHUB_HEAD_REF: ${{ github.head_ref }}\n          GITHUB_BASE_REF: ${{ github.base_ref }}\n        run: rust-script scripts/check-version-modification.rs\n\n  # === LINT AND FORMAT CHECK ===\n  # Lint runs independently of changelog check - it's a fast check that should always run\n  lint:\n    name: Lint and Format Check\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    needs: [detect-changes]\n    # Note: always() is required because detect-changes is skipped on workflow_dispatch,\n    # and without always(), this job would also be skipped even though its condition includes workflow_dispatch.\n    # See: https://github.com/actions/runner/issues/491\n    if: |\n      always() && !cancelled() && (\n        github.event_name == 'push' ||\n        github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' ||\n        needs.detect-changes.outputs.rs-changed == 'true' ||\n        needs.detect-changes.outputs.toml-changed == 'true' ||\n        needs.detect-changes.outputs.docs-changed == 'true' ||\n        needs.detect-changes.outputs.workflow-changed == 'true'\n      )\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v6\n\n      - name: Setup Rust\n        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable\n        with:\n          components: rustfmt, clippy\n\n      - name: Install rust-script\n        run: cargo install rust-script\n\n      - name: Cache cargo registry\n        uses: actions/cache@v5\n        with:\n          path: |\n            ~/.cargo/registry\n            ~/.cargo/git\n            target\n          key: ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}\n          restore-keys: |\n            ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-\n\n      - name: Check formatting\n        run: cargo fmt --all -- --check\n\n      - name: Run Clippy\n        run: cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features\n\n      - name: Check file size limit\n        run: rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs\n\n  # === TEST ===\n  # Test runs independently of changelog check\n  test:\n    name: Test (${{ matrix.os }})\n    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}\n    needs: [detect-changes, changelog]\n    # Run if: push event, OR changelog succeeded, OR changelog was skipped (docs-only PR)\n    if: always() && (github.event_name == 'push' || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' || needs.changelog.result == 'success' || needs.changelog.result == 'skipped')\n    strategy:\n      fail-fast: false\n      matrix:\n        os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest]\n    steps:\n      - name: Run tests\n        run: cargo test --all-features --verbose\n\n      - name: Run doc tests\n        run: cargo test --doc --verbose\n\n  # === BUILD ===\n  # Build package - only runs if lint and test pass\n  build:\n    name: Build Package\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    needs: [lint, test]\n    if: always() && !cancelled() && needs.lint.result == 'success' && needs.test.result == 'success'\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v6\n\n      - name: Setup Rust\n        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable\n\n      - name: Cache cargo registry\n        uses: actions/cache@v5\n        with:\n          path: |\n            ~/.cargo/registry\n            ~/.cargo/git\n            target\n          key: ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-build-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}\n          restore-keys: |\n            ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-build-\n\n      - name: Build release\n        run: cargo build --release --verbose\n\n      - name: Check package\n        run: cargo package --list\n\n  # === AUTO RELEASE ===\n  # Automatic release on push to main using changelog fragments\n  # This job automatically bumps version based on fragments in changelog.d/\n  auto-release:\n    name: Auto Release\n    needs: [lint, test, build]\n    # Note: always() ensures consistent behavior with other jobs that depend on jobs using always().\n    if: |\n      always() && !cancelled() &&\n      github.event_name == 'push' &&\n      github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' &&\n      needs.build.result == 'success'\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    permissions:\n      contents: write\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v6\n        with:\n          fetch-depth: 0\n          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Setup Rust\n        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable\n\n      - name: Install rust-script\n        run: cargo install rust-script\n\n      - name: Configure git\n        run: rust-script scripts/git-config.rs\n\n      - name: Determine bump type from changelog fragments\n        id: bump_type\n        run: rust-script scripts/get-bump-type.rs\n\n      - name: Check if version already released or no fragments\n        id: check\n        env:\n          HAS_FRAGMENTS: ${{ steps.bump_type.outputs.has_fragments }}\n        run: rust-script scripts/check-release-needed.rs\n\n      - name: Collect changelog and bump version\n        id: version\n        if: steps.check.outputs.should_release == 'true' && steps.check.outputs.skip_bump != 'true'\n        run: |\n          rust-script scripts/version-and-commit.rs \\\n            --bump-type \"${{ steps.bump_type.outputs.bump_type }}\"\n\n      - name: Get current version\n        id: current_version\n        if: steps.check.outputs.should_release == 'true'\n        run: rust-script scripts/get-version.rs\n\n      - name: Build release\n        if: steps.check.outputs.should_release == 'true'\n        run: cargo build --release\n\n      - name: Publish to Crates.io\n        if: steps.check.outputs.should_release == 'true'\n        id: publish-crate\n        env:\n          CARGO_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CARGO_TOKEN }}\n        run: rust-script scripts/publish-crate.rs\n\n      - name: Create GitHub Release\n        if: steps.check.outputs.should_release == 'true'\n        env:\n          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n        run: rust-script scripts/create-github-release.rs --release-version \"${{ steps.current_version.outputs.version }}\" --repository \"${{ github.repository }}\"\n\n  # === MANUAL INSTANT RELEASE ===\n  # Manual release via workflow_dispatch - only after CI passes\n  manual-release:\n    name: Instant Release\n    needs: [lint, test, build]\n    # Note: always() is required to evaluate the condition when dependencies use always().\n    # The build job ensures lint and test passed before this job runs.\n    if: |\n      always() && !cancelled() &&\n      github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' &&\n      github.event.inputs.release_mode == 'instant' &&\n      needs.build.result == 'success'\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    permissions:\n      contents: write\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v6\n        with:\n          fetch-depth: 0\n          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Setup Rust\n        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable\n\n      - name: Install rust-script\n        run: cargo install rust-script\n\n      - name: Configure git\n        run: rust-script scripts/git-config.rs\n\n      - name: Collect changelog fragments\n        run: rust-script scripts/collect-changelog.rs\n\n      - name: Version and commit\n        id: version\n        env:\n          BUMP_TYPE: ${{ github.event.inputs.bump_type }}\n          DESCRIPTION: ${{ github.event.inputs.description }}\n        run: rust-script scripts/version-and-commit.rs --bump-type \"${{ github.event.inputs.bump_type }}\" --description \"${{ github.event.inputs.description }}\"\n\n      - name: Build release\n        if: steps.version.outputs.version_committed == 'true' || steps.version.outputs.already_released == 'true'\n        run: cargo build --release\n\n      - name: Publish to Crates.io\n        if: steps.version.outputs.version_committed == 'true' || steps.version.outputs.already_released == 'true'\n        id: publish-crate\n        env:\n          CARGO_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CARGO_TOKEN }}\n        run: rust-script scripts/publish-crate.rs\n\n      - If there are changelog fragments to process\n//! 2. If the current version has already been published to crates.io\n//!\n//! IMPORTANT: This script checks crates.io (the source of truth for Rust packages),\n//! NOT git tags. This is critical because:\n//! - Git tags can exist without the package being published\n//! - GitHub releases create tags but don't publish to crates.io\n//! - Only crates.io publication means users can actually install the package\n//!\n//! Supports both single-language and multi-language repository structures:\n//! - Single-language: Cargo.toml in repository root\n//! - Multi-language: Cargo.toml in rust/ subfolder\n//!\n//! Usage: rust-script scripts/check-release-needed.rs [--rust-root <path>]\n//!\n//! Environment variables:\n//!   - HAS_FRAGMENTS: 'true' if changelog fragments exist (from get-bump-type.rs)\n//!\n//! Outputs (written to GITHUB_OUTPUT):\n//!   - should_release: 'true' if a release should be created\n//!   - skip_bump: 'true' if version bump should be skipped (version not yet released)\n//!\n//! ```cargo\n//! [dependencies]\n//! regex = \"1\"\n//! ureq = \"2\"\n//! serde = { version = \"1\", features = [\"derive\"] }\n//! serde_json = \"1\"\n//! ```\n\nuse std::env;\nuse std::fs;\nuse std::path::Path;\nuse std::process::exit;\nuse regex::Regex;\nuse serde::Deserialize;\n\nfn get_arg(name: &str) -> Option<String> {\n    let args: Vec<String> = env::args().collect();\n    let flag = format!(\"--{}\", name);\n\n    if let Some(idx) = args.iter().position(|a| a == &flag) {\n        return args.get(idx + 1).cloned();\n    }\n\n    let env_name = name.to_uppercase().replace('-', \"_\");\n    env::var(&env_name).ok().filter(|s| !s.is_empty())\n}\n\nfn get_rust_root() -> String {\n    if let Some(root) = get_arg(\"rust-root\") {\n        eprintln!(\"Using explicitly configured Rust root: {}\", root);\n        return root;\n    }\n\n    if Path::new(\"./Cargo.toml\").exists() {\n        eprintln!(\"Detected single-language repository (Cargo.toml in root)\");\n        return \".\".to_string();\n    }\n\n    if Path::new(\"./rust/Cargo.toml\").exists() {\n        eprintln!(\"Detected multi-language repository (Cargo.toml in rust/)\");\n        return \"rust\".to_string();\n    }\n\n    eprintln!(\"Error: Could not find Cargo.toml in expected locations\");\n    exit(1);\n}\n\nfn get_cargo_toml_path(rust_root: &str) -> String {\n    if rust_root == \".\" {\n        \"./Cargo.toml\".to_string()\n    } else {\n        format!(\"{}/Cargo.toml\", rust_root)\n    }\n}\n\nfn set_output(key: &str, value: &str) {\n    if let Ok(output_file) = env::var(\"GITHUB_OUTPUT\") {\n        if let Err(e) = fs::OpenOptions::new()\n            .create(true)\n            .append(true)\n            .open(&output_file)\n            .and_then(|mut f| {\n                use std::io::Write;\n                writeln!(f, \"{}={}\", key, value)\n            })\n        {\n            eprintln!(\"Warning: Could not write to GITHUB_OUTPUT: {}\", e);\n        }\n    }\n    println!(\"Output: {}={}\", key, value);\n}\n\nfn get_current_version(cargo_toml_path: &str) -> Result<String, String> {\n    let content = fs::read_to_string(cargo_toml_path)\n        .map_err(|e| format!(\"Failed to read {}: {}\", cargo_toml_path, e))?;\n\n    let re = Regex::new(r#\"(?m)^version\\s*=\\s*\"([^\"]+)\"\"#).unwrap();\n\n    if let Some(caps) = re.captures(&content) {\n        Ok(caps.get(1).unwrap().as_str().to_string())\n    } else {\n        Err(format!(\"Could not find version in {}\", cargo_toml_path))\n    }\n}\n\nfn get_crate_name(cargo_toml_path: &str) -> Result<String, String> {\n    let content = fs::read_to_string(cargo_toml_path)\n        .map_err(|e| format!(\"Failed to read {}: {}\", cargo_toml_path, e))?;\n\n    let re = Regex::new(r#\"(?m)^name\\s*=\\s*\"([^\"]+)\"\"#).unwrap();\n\n    if let Some(caps) = re.captures(&content) {\n        Ok(caps.get(1).unwrap().as_str().to_string())\n    } else {\n        Err(format!(\"Could not find name in {}\", cargo_toml_path))\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Deserialize)]\nstruct CratesIoVersion {\n    version: -> bool {\n    let url = format!(\"https://crates.io/api/v1/crates/{}/{}\", crate_name, version);\n\n    match ureq::get(&url)\n        .set(\"User-Agent\", \"rust-script-check-release\")\n        .call()\n    {\n        Ok(response) => {\n            if response.status() == 200 {\n                // Version exists on crates.io\n                if let Ok(body) = response.into_string() {\n                    if let Ok(data) = serde_json::from_str::<CratesIoVersion>(&body) {\n                        return data.version.is_some();\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            false\n        }\n        Err(ureq::Error::Status(404, _)) => {\n            // Version doesn't exist on crates.io\n            false\n        }\n        Err(e) => {\n            eprintln!(\"Warning: Could not check crates.io: {}\", e);\n            // On error, assume not published (safer than incorrectly skipping)\n            false\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nfn main() {\n    let rust_root = get_rust_root();\n    let cargo_toml = get_cargo_toml_path(&rust_root);\n\n    let has_fragments = env::var(\"HAS_FRAGMENTS\")\n        .map(|v| v == \"true\")\n        .unwrap_or(false);\n\n    if !has_fragments {\n        // No fragments - check if current version is published on crates.io\n        let crate_name = match get_crate_name(&cargo_toml) {\n            Ok(name) => name,\n            Err(e) => {\n                eprintln!(\"Error: {}\", e);\n                exit(1);\n            }\n        };\n\n        let current_version = match get_current_version(&cargo_toml) {\n            Ok(version) => version,\n            Err(e) => {\n                eprintln!(\"Error: {}\", e);\n                exit(1);\n            }\n        };\n\n        let is_published = check_version_on_crates_io(&crate_name, &current_version);\n\n        println!(\n            \"Crate: {}, Version: {}, Published on crates.io: {}\",\n            crate_name, current_version, is_published\n        );\n\n        if is_published {\n            println!(\n                \"No changelog fragments and v{} already published on crates.io\",\n                current_version\n            );\n            set_output(\"should_release\", \"false\");\n        } else {\n            println!(\n                \"No changelog fragments but v{} not yet published to crates.io\",\n                current_version\n            );\n            set_output(\"should_release\", \"true\");\n            set_output(\"skip_bump\", \"true\");\n        }\n    } else {\n        println!(\"Found changelog fragments, proceeding with release\");\n        set_output(\"should_release\", \"true\");\n        set_output(\"skip_bump\", \"false\");\n    }\n}",

IMPORTANT: This script checks crates.io (the source of truth for Rust packages),\n//! NOT git tags. This is critical because:\n//! - Git tags can exist without the package being published\n//! - GitHub releases create tags but don't publish to crates.io\n//! - Only crates.io publication means users can actually install the package\n//!\n//! This script reads changeset fragments from changelog.d/ and determines\n//! the version bump type based on the frontmatter in each fragment.\n//!\n//! Supports both single-language and multi-language repository structures:\n//! - Single-language: changelog.d/ in repository root\n//! - Multi-language: changelog.d/ in rust/ subfolder\n//!\n//! Fragment format:\n//! ---\n//! bump: patch|minor|major\n//! ---\n//!\n//! ### Added\n//! - Your changes here\n//!\n//! Usage: rust-script scripts/get-bump-type.rs [--default <patch|minor|major>] [--rust-root <path>]\n//!\n//! ```cargo\n//! [dependencies]\n//! regex = \"1\"\n//! ```\n\nuse std::env;\nuse std::fs;\nuse std::io::Write;\nuse std::path::Path;\nuse std::process::exit;\nuse regex::Regex;\n\nfn get_arg(name: &str) -> Option<String> {\n    let args: Vec<String> = env::args().collect();\n    let flag = format!(\"--{}\", name);\n\n    if let Some(idx) = args.iter().position(|a| a == &flag) {\n        return args.get(idx + 1).cloned();\n    }\n\n    let env_name = name.to_uppercase().replace('-', \"_\");\n    env::var(&env_name).ok().filter(|s| !s.is_empty())\n}\n\nfn get_rust_root() -> String {\n    if let Some(root) = get_arg(\"rust-root\") {\n        eprintln!(\"Using explicitly configured Rust root: {}\", root);\n        return root;\n    }\n\n    if Path::new(\"./Cargo.toml\").exists() {\n        eprintln!(\"Detected single-language repository (Cargo.toml in root)\");\n        return \".\".to_string();\n    }\n\n    if Path::new(\"./rust/Cargo.toml\").exists() {\n        eprintln!(\"Detected multi-language repository (Cargo.toml in rust/)\");\n        return \"rust\".to_string();\n    }\n\n    eprintln!(\"Error: Could not find Cargo.toml in expected locations\");\n    exit(1);\n}\n\nfn get_changelog_dir(rust_root: &str) -> String {\n    if rust_root == \".\" {\n        \"./changelog.d\".to_string()\n    } else {\n        format!(\"{}/changelog.d\", rust_root)\n    }\n}\n\nfn set_output(key: &str, value: &str) {\n    if let Ok(output_file) = env::var(\"GITHUB_OUTPUT\") {\n        if let Ok(mut file) = fs::OpenOptions::new().create(true).append(true).open(&output_file) {\n            let _ = writeln!(file, \"{}={}\", key, value);\n        }\n    }\n    println!(\"Output: {}={}\", key, value);\n}\n\nfn bump_priority(bump_type: &str) -> u8 {\n    match bump_type {\n        \"patch\" => 1,\n        \"minor\" => 2,\n        \"major\" => 3,\n        _ => 0,\n    }\n}\n\nfn parse_frontmatter(content: &str) -> Option<String> {\n    let re = Regex::new(r\"(?s)^---\\s*\\n(.*?)\\n---\").unwrap();\n\n    if let Some(caps) = re.captures(content) {\n        let frontmatter = caps.get(1).unwrap().as_str();\n\n        // Parse YAML-like frontmatter (simple key: value format)\n        for line in frontmatter.lines() {\n            let bump_re = Regex::new(r\"^\\s*bump\\s*:\\s*(.+?)\\s*$\").unwrap();\n            if let Some(bump_caps) = bump_re.captures(line) {\n                return Some(bump_caps.get(1).unwrap().as_str().to_string());\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    None\n}\n\nfn determine_bump_type(changelog_dir: &str, default_bump: &str) -> (String, usize) {\n    let dir_path = Path::new(changelog_dir);\n    if !dir_path.exists() {\n        println!(\"No {} directory found\", changelog_dir);\n        return (default_bump.to_string(), 0);\n    }\n\n    let mut files: Vec<_> = match fs::read_dir(dir_path) {\n        Ok(entries) => entries\n            .filter_map(|e| e.ok())\n            .map(|e| e.path())\n            .filter(|p| {\n                p.extension().map_or(false, |ext| ext == \"md\")\n                    && p.file_name().map_or(false, |name| name != \"README.md\")\n            })\n            .collect(),\n        Err(_) => {\n            println!(\"No changelog fragments found\");\n            return (default_bump.to_string(), 0);\n        }\n    };\n\n    if files.is_empty() {\n        println!(\"No changelog fragments found\");\n        return (default_bump.to_string(), 0);\n    }\n\n    files.sort();\n\n    let mut highest_priority: u8 = 0;\n    let mut highest_bump_type = default_bump.to_string();\n\n    for file in &files {\n        if let Ok(content) = fs::read_to_string(file) {\n            if let Some(bump) = parse_frontmatter(&content) {\n                let priority = bump_priority(&bump);\n                if priority > highest_priority {\n                    highest_priority = priority;\n                    highest_bump_type = bump.clone();\n                }\n                println!(\"Fragment {}: bump={}\", file.file_name().unwrap().to_string_lossy(), bump);\n            } else {\n                println!(\n                    \"Fragment {}: no bump specified, using default\",\n                    file.file_name().unwrap().to_string_lossy()\n                );\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    (highest_bump_type, files.len())\n}\n\nfn main() {\n    let default_bump = get_arg(\"default\").unwrap_or_else(|| \"patch\".to_string());\n    let rust_root = get_rust_root();\n    let changelog_dir = get_changelog_dir(&rust_root);\n\n    let (bump_type, fragment_count) = determine_bump_type(&changelog_dir, &default_bump);\n\n    println!(\"\\nDetermined bump type: {} (from {} fragment(s))\", bump_type, fragment_count);\n\n    set_output(\"bump_type\", &bump_type);\n    set_output(\"fragment_count\", &fragment_count.to_string());\n    set_output(\"has_fragments\", if fragment_count > 0 { \"true\" } else { \"false\" });\n}",

Regex::new(r\"(?s)^---\\s*\\n(.*?)\\n---\").unwrap();\n\n    if let Some(caps) = re.captures(content) {\n        let frontmatter = caps.get(1).unwrap().as_str();\n\n        // Parse YAML-like frontmatter (simple key: value format)\n        for line in frontmatter.lines() {\n            let bump_re = Regex::new(r\"^\\s*bump\\s*:\\s*(.+?)\\s*$\").unwrap();\n            if let Some(bump_caps) = bump_re.captures(line) {\n                return Some(bump_caps.get(1).unwrap().as_str().to_string());\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    None\n}\n\nfn determine_bump_type(changelog_dir: &str, default_bump: &str) -> (String, usize) {\n    let dir_path = Path::new(changelog_dir);\n    if !dir_path.exists() {\n        println!(\"No {} directory found\", changelog_dir);\n        return (default_bump.to_string(), 0);\n    }\n\n    let mut files: Vec<_> = match fs::read_dir(dir_path) {\n        Ok(entries) => entries\n            .filter_map(|e| e.ok())\n            .map(|e| e.path())\n            .filter(|p| {\n                p.extension().map_or(false, |ext| ext == \"md\")\n                    && p.file_name().map_or(false, |name| name != \"README.md\")\n            })\n            .collect(),\n        Err(_) => {\n            println!(\"No changelog fragments found\");\n            return (default_bump.to_string(), 0);\n        }\n    };\n\n    if files.is_empty() {\n        println!(\"No changelog fragments found\");\n        return (default_bump.to_string(), 0);\n    }\n\n    files.sort();\n\n    let mut highest_priority: u8 = 0;\n    let mut highest_bump_type = default_bump.to_string();\n\n    for file in &files {\n        if let Ok(content) = fs::read_to_string(file) {\n            if let Some(bump) = parse_frontmatter(&content) {\n                let priority = bump_priority(&bump);\n                if priority > highest_priority {\n                    highest_priority = priority;\n                    highest_bump_type = bump.clone();\n                }\n                println!(\"Fragment {}: bump={}\", file.file_name().unwrap().to_string_lossy(), bump);\n            } else {\n                println!(\n                    \"Fragment {}: no bump specified, using default\",\n                    file.file_name().unwrap().to_string_lossy()\n                );\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    (highest_bump_type, files.len())\n}\n\nfn main() {\n    let default_bump = get_arg(\"default\").unwrap_or_else(|| \"patch\".to_string());\n    let rust_root = get_rust_root();\n    let changelog_dir = get_changelog_dir(&rust_root);\n\n    let (bump_type, fragment_count) = determine_bump_type(&changelog_dir, &default_bump);\n\n    println!(\"\\nDetermined bump type: {} (from {} fragment(s))\", bump_type, fragment_count);\n\n    set_output(\"bump_type\", &bump_type);\n    set_output(\"fragment_count\", &fragment_count.to_string());\n    set_output(\"has_fragments\", if fragment_count > 0 { \"true\" } else { \"false\" });\n}",
    "stderr": "",
    "interrupted": false,
    "isImage": false,
    "noOutputExpected": false
  }
}

std::path::Path;\nuse std::process::{Command, Stdio, exit};\nuse regex::Regex;\nuse serde::Serialize;\n\nfn get_arg(name: &str) -> Option<String> {\n    let args: Vec<String> = env::args().collect();\n    let flag = format!(\"--{}\", name);\n\n    if let Some(idx) = args.iter().position(|a| a == &flag) {\n        return args.get(idx + 1).cloned();\n    }\n\n    let env_name = name.to_uppercase().replace('-', \"_\");\n    env::var(&env_name).ok().filter(|s| !s.is_empty())\n}\n\nfn get_changelog_for_version(version: &str) -> String {\n    let changelog_path = \"CHANGELOG.md\";\n\n    if !Path::new(changelog_path).exists() {\n        return format!(\"Release v{}\", version);\n    }\n\n    let content = match fs::read_to_string(changelog_path) {\n        Ok(c) => c,\n        Err(_) => return format!(\"Release v{}\", version),\n    };\n\n    // Find the section for this version\n    let escaped_version = regex::escape(version);\n    let pattern = format!(r\"(?s)## \\[{}\\].*?\\n(.*?)(?=\\n## \\[|$)\", escaped_version);\n    let re = Regex::new(&pattern).unwrap();\n\n    if let Some(caps) = re.captures(&content) {\n        caps.get(1).unwrap().as_str().trim().to_string()\n    } else {\n        format!(\"Release v{}\", version)\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Serialize)]\nstruct ReleasePayload {\n    tag_name: String,\n    name: String,\n    body: String,\n}\n\nfn main() {\n    let version = match get_arg(\"release-version\") {\n        Some(v) => v,\n        None => {\n            eprintln!(\"Error: Missing required argument --release-version\");\n            eprintln!(\"Usage: rust-script scripts/create-github-release.rs --release-version <version> --repository <repository>\");\n            exit(1);\n        }\n    };\n\n    let repository = match get_arg(\"repository\") {\n        Some(r) => r,\n        None => {\n            eprintln!(\"Error: Missing required argument --repository\");\n            eprintln!(\"Usage: rust-script scripts/create-github-release.rs --release-version <version> --repository <repository>\");\n            exit(1);\n        }\n    };\n\n    let tag_prefix = get_arg(\"tag-prefix\").unwrap_or_else(|| \"v\".to_string());\n    let crates_io_url = get_arg(\"crates-io-url\");\n\n    let tag = format!(\"{}{}\", tag_prefix, version);\n    println!(\"Creating GitHub release for {}...\", tag);\n\n    let mut release_notes = get_changelog_for_version(&version);\n\n    // Add crates.io link if provided\n    if let Some(url) = crates_io_url {\n        release_notes = format!(\"{}\\n\\n{}\", url, release_notes);\n    }\n\n    // Create release using GitHub API with JSON input\n    let payload = ReleasePayload {\n        tag_name: tag.clone(),\n        name: format!(\"{}{}\", tag_prefix, version),\n        body: release_notes,\n    };\n\n    let payload_json = serde_json::to_string(&payload).expect(\"Failed to serialize payload\");\n\n    let mut This script reads the version from Cargo.toml and outputs it\n//! for use in GitHub Actions.\n//!\n//! Supports both single-language and multi-language repository structures:\n//! - Single-language: Cargo.toml in repository root\n//! - Multi-language: Cargo.toml in rust/ subfolder\n//!\n//! Usage: rust-script scripts/get-version.rs [--rust-root <path>]\n//!\n//! Outputs (written to GITHUB_OUTPUT):\n//!   - version: The current version from Cargo.toml\n//!\n//! ```cargo\n//! [dependencies]\n//! regex = \"1\"\n//! ```\n\nuse std::env;\nuse std::fs;\nuse std::path::Path;\nuse std::process::exit;\nuse regex::Regex;\n\nfn get_rust_root() -> String {\n    // Check CLI arguments\n    let args: Vec<String> = env::args().collect();\n    if let Some(idx) = args.iter().position(|a| a == \"--rust-root\") {\n        if let Some(root) = args.get(idx + 1) {\n }\n\n    // Auto-detect\n    if Path::new(\"./Cargo.toml\").exists() {\n        eprintln!(\"Detected single-language repository (Cargo.toml in root)\");\n        return \".\".to_string();\n    }\n\n Could not find Cargo.toml in expected locations\");\n    exit(1);\n}\n\nfn get_cargo_toml_path(rust_root: &str) -> String {\n    if rust_root == \".\" {\n        \"./Cargo.toml\".to_string()\n    } else {\n        format!(\"{}/Cargo.toml\", rust_root)\n    }\n}\n\nfn set_output(key: &str, value: &str) {\n    if let Ok(output_file) = env::var(\"GITHUB_OUTPUT\") {\n        if let Err(e) =         "name": "Write",
        "input": {
          "file_path": "/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1774738312148/docs/case-studies/issue-20/README.md",
          "content": "# Case Study: Issue #20 — CI/CD Version Regression and Release Failure\n\n## Overview\n\n**Issue:** [#20 — Something wrong with our CI/CD again](https://github.com/linksplatform/trees-rs/issues/20)\n**Affected Run:** [Run 23695940118](https://github.com/linksplatform/trees-rs/actions/runs/23695940118/job/69031223565)\n**Date:** 2026-03-28\n**Severity:** High — blocked automated releases\n\n---\n\n## Timeline of Events\n\n| Time (UTC) | Event |\n|---|---|\n| ~2026-03-24 | CI/CD run fails with \"Could not parse version\" (referenced in issue but not the specific log we analyzed) |\n| 2026-03-28T21:20 | Tag `v0.2.0` and GitHub release `v0.2.0` created (unclear how — likely a prior successful bump or manual action) |\n| 2026-03-28T22:12 | GitHub release `v0.1.0-beta.1` created (CI created release for old stale version) |\n| 2026-03-28T22:40 | Run 23695940118 starts on commit `4e1333a` (push to main) |\n| 2026-03-28T22:43 | get-bump-type.rs detects 6 changelog fragments → bump type: `minor` |\n| 2026-03-28T22:44 | check-release-needed.rs: `should_release=true`, `skip_bump=false` |\n| 2026-03-28T22:44 | version-and-commit.rs runs, computes target version `0.2.0`, detects git tag `v0.2.0` already exists → sets `already_released=true`, `new_version=0.2.0`, exits **without updating Cargo.toml** |\n| 2026-03-28T22:44 | get-version.rs reads stale Cargo.toml → outputs `version=0.1.0-beta.1` |\n| 2026-03-28T22:44 | publish-crate.rs: \"Version 0.1.0-beta.1 already exists on crates.io\" — skips (OK) |\n| 2026-03-28T22:44 | create-github-release.rs: tries to create release for `v0.1.0-beta.1` → HTTP 422 \"Validation Failed\" |\n| 2026-03-28T22:44 | Error check only looks for `\"already exists\"` in stderr, doesn't match 422 body → **exits with code 1** |\n| 2026-03-28T22:44 | Auto Release job **FAILS** |\n\n---\n\n## Root Causes\n\n### Root Cause 1: Stale Cargo.toml version (version regression)\n\n**What happened:**\nThe git tag `v0.2.0` was created (by a prior CI run or manual action), but `Cargo.toml` still contained `version = \"0.1.0-beta.1\"`. The automated pipeline then detected this inconsistency.\n\n**Why it happened:**\n`version-and-commit.rs` bumps Cargo.toml and creates the tag atomically. If the process was interrupted or a tag was created externally without updating Cargo.toml (e.g., via manual `git tag`), Cargo.toml and git tags can diverge.\n\n**Evidence:**\n- `Cargo.toml` contains `version = \"0.1.0-beta.1\"` on the main branch\n- `git tag` shows both `v0.1.0-beta.1` and `v0.2.0`\n- `gh release list` shows both releases\n\n### Root Cause 2: Wrong version passed to create-github-release in auto-release job\n\n**What happened:**\nWhen `version-and-commit.rs` detects `already_released=true` (tag exists), it does NOT update Cargo.toml. However, the subsequent `get-version.rs` step reads the stale Cargo.toml and outputs the old version. The `Create GitHub Release` step in auto-release used `steps.current_version.outputs.version` (from `get-version.rs`) instead of `steps.version.outputs.new_version` (from `version-and-commit.rs`).\n\n**Contrast:** The manual-release job correctly uses `steps.version.outputs.new_version` throughout.\n\n**Evidence (from CI log lines 3782-3797):**\n```\nversion-and-commit.rs: Tag v0.2.0 already exists\nversion-and-commit.rs: Output: already_released=true\nversion-and-commit.rs: Output: new_version=0.2.0\n\nget-version.rs: Current version: 0.1.0-beta.1\nget-version.rs: Output: version=0.1.0-beta.1\n\ncreate-github-release.rs: Creating GitHub release for v0.1.0-beta.1...\nError creating release: gh: Validation Failed (HTTP 422)\n```\n\n### Root Cause 3: Insufficient error handling in create-github-release.rs\n\n**What happened:**\n`create-github-release.rs` only checks for `\"already exists\"` in stderr to detect a duplicate release. But when GitHub's API returns HTTP 422, the `gh` CLI outputs `\"Validation Failed (HTTP 422)\"` — not `\"already exists\"`.\n\n**Fix applied:** Expanded the check to also match `\"Validation Failed\"` in the combined stderr+stdout output.\n\n---\n\n## Solutions Implemented\n\n### Fix 1: Restore Cargo.toml version to match released tag\n\nUpdated `Cargo.toml` version from `0.1.0-beta.1` → `0.2.0` to match the already-released `v0.2.0` tag.\n\n### Fix 2: Use correct version in auto-release Create GitHub Release step\n\nIn `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, the auto-release `Create GitHub Release` step now uses `steps.version.outputs.new_version` (computed by `version-and-commit.rs`, which checks git tags) as the authoritative version, falling back to `steps.current_version.outputs.version` only if not available.\n\nThis makes auto-release consistent with manual-release, which already used `new_version`.\n\n### Fix 3: Robust error handling in create-github-release.rs\n\nExpanded the \"release already exists\" check to catch the actual GitHub API error message `\"Validation Failed\"` (HTTP 422), in addition to the literal `\"already exists\"` string.\n\n---\n\n## Prevention\n\nTo prevent similar issues in the future:\n\n1. **Never create git tags manually** without also updating `Cargo.toml` — let the CI pipeline do it atomically.\n2. **Add a consistency check** step that validates that `version` in `Cargo.toml` matches the latest git tag (could be added to lint or a separate check step).\n3. **Prefer authoritative sources** — `version-and-commit.rs` is the authoritative source for the release version during a CI run; always use its output, not re-reads of Cargo.toml.\n\n---\n\n## Artifacts\n\n- CI run log: [`ci-logs/run-23695940118.log`](../../../ci-logs/run-23695940118.log)\n- Affected files: `Cargo.toml`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `scripts/create-github-release.rs`\n"
        },
        "caller": {
          "type": "direct"
        }
      }

Auto-release used wrong version for GitHub release creation\nThe auto-release job read the stale Cargo.toml version via `get-version.rs` instead of using the tag-checked `new_version` output from `version-and-commit.rs`. This caused the job to try creating a release for `v0.1.0-beta.1` (already existed) instead of `v0.2.0`. Fixed in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — now uses `steps.version.outputs.new_version` (consistent with how manual-release already worked).\n\n### 3. GitHub 422 error not handled gracefully\n`create-github-release.rs` only checked for `\"already exists\"` string, but GitHub's API returns `\"Validation Failed (HTTP 422)\"`.
